problem: The login page is rendered inside the full sidebar/topbar app shell before authentication.
impact: Users see primary navigation and app chrome before sign-in, which crowds the auth experience and weakens IA clarity.
recommendation: Add an auth route group/layout exception or conditionally omit sidebar/topbar on auth routes.
acceptanceCriteria: The login route displays an auth-focused shell.
Primary app navigation is not shown before sign-in.
The auth shell still uses locked RadioPad tokens.

evidence.notes: Root layout wraps all children in AppShell; login renders a form within rp-container.

problem: Shared tables have no built-in responsive behavior and many pages render dense tables directly.
impact: Mobile and tablet users are likely to encounter horizontal overflow or unreadable compressed columns.
recommendation: Introduce a canonical ResponsiveTable/TableFrame component using locked tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: No page-level horizontal overflow at 320px, 360px, 390px, 430px, or 768px.
Tables expose accessible headers and actions.
Dense rows have a mobile card or horizontal-scroll fallback.

problem: Closed mobile sidebar appears to be moved off-screen rather than made inert, and no focus trap/return behavior was found.
impact: Keyboard and assistive-technology users may tab into hidden navigation or lose focus context.
recommendation: Implement accessible drawer behavior: inert when closed, focus on open, focus trap, Escape close, focus return.
acceptanceCriteria: Closed drawer has no focusable descendants.
Opening the drawer moves focus into it.
Escape closes the drawer and returns focus to the menu button.

problem: Provider and template modals do not consistently implement dialog semantics, initial focus, focus trap, Escape close, and focus return.
impact: Keyboard users may be unable to reliably complete or exit modal workflows.
recommendation: Create and use a shared accessible Dialog primitive.
acceptanceCriteria: Dialogs have accessible names and aria-modal semantics.
Focus is trapped while open.
Focus returns to the opener on close.

evidence.notes: Provider modal lacks role=dialog/aria-modal; template modal is partial.

problem: Many visible labels are not programmatically associated with controls.
impact: Screen-reader users may not know what inputs, selects, and textareas represent.
recommendation: Add stable id/htmlFor pairs or wrap controls inside labels.
acceptanceCriteria: Every form control has a programmatic label.
Automated accessibility checks do not report unlabeled controls.
Label text remains visually consistent with locked CSS.

evidence.notes: Examples found in login and report editor section-block patterns.

problem: Report editor toolbar presents many equal-weight actions for AI generation, rewrite, validation, export, copy, and acknowledgement.
impact: The primary clinical workflow is visually diluted and harder to learn.
recommendation: Group actions by workflow stage and reserve primary styling for the next safe step.
acceptanceCriteria: Only one primary action is visible per workflow surface.
Exports are grouped behind a menu or secondary panel.
Disabled actions explain why they are disabled.

evidence.notes: Toolbar actions span ReportClient source around the report editing surface.

problem: The editor uses a fixed split layout with a 380px-460px first column and no mobile media query.
impact: The editor can overflow or become unusable on mobile/tablet widths.
recommendation: Add responsive stacking for split editor layouts.
acceptanceCriteria: No horizontal overflow at 320px and 768px.
YAML preview and visual editor remain reachable.
Primary save/validate actions remain visible.

problem: Prompt Studio uses generic pane/panel classes outside the canonical Container/PageHeader/rp-panel system.
impact: Visual styling can drift or be missing, and the page does not follow the locked shell pattern.
recommendation: Rebuild the page structure with Container, PageHeader, rp-panel, and documented tab patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: No undefined panel/panel-header classes are used.
The page uses canonical shell/page primitives.
Tabs are accessible and visually consistent.

problem: Sign-in copy exposes developer/internal implementation details.
impact: Production users see confusing and low-trust technical language.
recommendation: Use user-facing identity copy and move diagnostics to a dev/admin help area.
acceptanceCriteria: Login copy does not mention headers, reverse proxies, or token storage.
Dev details are hidden behind explicit diagnostics/help.
Copy remains localizable.

evidence.notes: Source copy references dev identity and infrastructure details.

problem: Mobile offline drafts can fall back to Capacitor Preferences/localStorage for report content.
impact: PHI-bearing draft storage may not match user expectations for secure native offline storage.
recommendation: Require encrypted mobile draft storage or document/gate platform security controls with clear UI state.
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile offline draft storage is encrypted or explicitly disabled/gated.
Users can see whether local drafts are protected.
Unsupported storage paths show clear error/recovery copy.

evidence.notes: Native shell audit reviewed offlineDrafts and mobile fallback behavior.

problem: Mobile backend URL handoff is not defined at runtime unless NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE is baked into the export.
impact: Capacitor users can land in unclear offline/API failure states.
recommendation: Define a mobile backend base URL strategy for dev, staging, production, offline, and invalid certificates.
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile app can resolve backend URL in each target environment.
Invalid or unreachable backend states show actionable UI.
No PHI is exposed in failure copy.

evidence.notes: api.ts falls back to Tauri bridge or empty base; Capacitor config only documents production assumption.
